text.title: Customer Success Metrics and Health Scoring Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `ops-customer-success-metrics` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Compute a per-account health score that combines at least four dimensions: usage frequency, feature adoption breadth, support sentiment, and billing status. Single-dimension scores (usage only) are forbidden.

rationale.source: Customer success operations practice (Gainsight / ChurnZero playbooks)

rule.rationale: Single-dimension health scores miss the leading indicators (sentiment drop, billing failure, declining feature adoption) that precede churn. Multi-dimensional scoring matches the multi-causal nature of churn.

rule.statement: Track retention as cohort curves by signup month (or onboarding cohort), not as a single aggregated rate. Aggregated churn rate hides cohort-level decay.

rationale.source: SaaS metrics canon (David Skok, Jason Lemkin)

rule.rationale: Aggregated churn rate mixes mature and immature cohorts, hiding both improvements and degradations. Cohort curves expose where retention actually changes.

rule.statement: Configure alerts that fire BEFORE churn — usage drop > 25% week-over-week, ≥3 support tickets in 7 days, billing failure. Alert on lagging indicators only (churn already happened) is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Lagging-indicator alerts arrive after the save window has closed. Leading indicators give CSMs a chance to intervene.

rule.statement: Run NPS / CSAT surveys on a fixed cadence (quarterly NPS, post-interaction CSAT) and tie response scores back to the health-score model. Ad-hoc surveys without integration into the score are research-theatre.
rule.rationale: Surveys disconnected from the operating health score produce vanity numbers that no one acts on. Tying them to the score forces consequence.

rule.statement: Report Net Revenue Retention (NRR) and Gross Revenue Retention (GRR) monthly. Expansion ARR is reported separately from retention so the two motions are not conflated.
rule.rationale: Conflating expansion and retention hides whether the business is retaining customers vs growing share-of-wallet on a shrinking base.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
